extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (IF bradycardia AND immunosuppressed). rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (IF bradycardia). Priority/tie resolution is per-output, so the two rules govern different outputs and never compete; each fires independently. With bradycardia=true and immunosuppressed=true, rec_b_from_trigger sets rec_drug_b:=true (bradycardia holds) and rec_a_from_conjunction sets rec_drug_a:=true (both hold). The defaults never apply to these outputs because both are set. Thus rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). diabetes is irrelevant (only affects rec_drug_c); set to false for a minimal witness.
